title: Formspec Screener Document
description: A standalone Formspec Screener document for respondent classification and routing. A Screener is a freestanding routing instrument — it does not bind to a target Definition. Its relationship to Definitions is expressed entirely through route targets. A Screener declares screening items, binds evaluated in an isolated scope, and an ordered evaluation pipeline with pluggable strategies (first-match, fan-out, score-threshold, or extensions). Evaluation produces a Determination Record capturing matched routes, eliminated routes, scores, inputs with answer states, and evaluation metadata. Lifecycle primitives: availability windows, result validity durations, and evaluation version binding.

x-lm.intent: Version pin for screener document compatibility. Processors MUST reject screeners with an unrecognized version.

url.description: Canonical, stable URI identifying this screener. MUST be globally unique. The pair (url, version) uniquely identifies a specific screener revision. Does not need to be a resolvable HTTP URL; URN syntax is acceptable.

x-lm.intent: Stable identifier of the screener across versions.

version.description: Semantic version of this Screener Document, following semver 2.0.0. Independent of any Definition version.

x-lm.intent: Screener revision identifier. Enables auditing which screener version produced a given Determination Record.
Display name shown to respondents and in authoring tools.

examples: Determines applicant eligibility and routes to the appropriate grant form.
Evaluates household eligibility across all assistance programs.

availability.description: Calendar window during which the screener accepts new respondents. When omitted, the screener is always available.

resultValidity.description: ISO 8601 duration declaring how long a completed Determination Record remains valid before re-screening is required. When omitted, the Determination Record has no expiration.

evaluationBinding.description: Determines which version of the screener's evaluation logic governs when the screener is updated between session start and completion. 'submission': rules at session start govern (default). 'completion': rules at completion govern.

items.description: Screening items. Uses the standard Formspec Item schema (core §4.2). Screener items are NOT part of any form's instance data — they exist only for routing classification. Item keys MUST be unique within the Screener Document.

x-lm.intent: The data collection elements of the screener. Same schema as Definition items but evaluated in an isolated scope.

binds.description: Bind declarations scoped to screener items. Uses the standard Formspec Bind schema (core §4.3). Paths reference screener item keys. These binds are evaluated in the screener's own scope — they do NOT interact with any Definition's binds.

evaluation.description: Ordered evaluation pipeline. Phases execute in declaration order. Override routes are hoisted and evaluated before all phases.

x-lm.intent: The routing logic of the screener. Each phase declares a strategy and a set of routes.

extensions.description: Extension declarations. Uses the same extension mechanism as Definition (core §4.6).

Availability.description: Calendar window during which the screener accepts new respondents. Either or both properties may be omitted for open-ended windows.

from.description: Earliest date (inclusive) on which the screener accepts respondents. If omitted, no start constraint.

until.description: Latest date (inclusive) on which the screener accepts respondents. If omitted, no end constraint.

Phase.description: A single stage in the evaluation pipeline. Each phase declares a strategy that determines how its routes are evaluated. Phases execute in declaration order and produce independent results aggregated into the Determination Record.

id.description: Unique identifier for this phase within the Screener.

strategy.description: Evaluation strategy. Normative values: 'first-match', 'fan-out', 'score-threshold'. Extension strategies MUST use the 'x-' prefix.

x-lm.intent: Determines how routes in this phase are evaluated. Core conformance requires 'first-match'. Complete conformance requires all three normative strategies.

routes.description: Routes to evaluate using this phase's strategy.

activeWhen.description: When present, the phase is evaluated only when this expression evaluates to true. When absent, the phase always evaluates.

config.description: Strategy-specific configuration. Normative strategies define their own config schemas. Extension strategies define their own.

minMatches.description: Fan-out: minimum routes that must match for success.

maxMatches.description: Fan-out: maximum matched routes to include.

topN.description: Score-threshold: return only the top N scoring routes.

normalize.description: Score-threshold: when true, normalize scores to 0.0-1.0 range before threshold comparison.

Route.description: A single routing rule within an evaluation phase. Routes combine a condition or score expression with a target destination. Override routes are hoisted out of their phase and evaluated before all phases.

condition.description: Boolean FEL expression evaluated against screener item values. Required for 'first-match' and 'fan-out' strategies.

score.description: Numeric FEL expression evaluated against screener item values. Required for 'score-threshold' strategy.

threshold.description: Minimum score required for this route to match (score >= threshold). Required for 'score-threshold' strategy.

target.description: Route destination URI. May be a Formspec Definition reference (url|version), an external URI, or a named outcome (outcome:name).

x-lm.intent: Where the respondent is directed when this route matches. Three categories: Definition references, external URIs, and named outcomes.

message.description: Human-readable message to display to the respondent when this route matches. MAY contain {{expression}} interpolation sequences.
examples: Your household likely qualifies for SNAP benefits.
Based on your responses, you do not meet the eligibility criteria.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the route. Preserved in the Determination Record without interpretation by the processor.

override.description: When true, this route is an override route. Override routes are hoisted out of their phase and evaluated before all phases.

terminal.description: When true and the override matches, the entire evaluation pipeline halts. Ignored when 'override' is false.
